rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers used in assumptions and guarantees are visible in the current AADL scope and properly declared.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent type mismatches and unresolved references.
Ensure that guarantee expressions relate visible inputs to visible outputs without relying on implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Validate that all referenced identifiers in AGREE code are present in the AADL model or explicitly declared within the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax requires explicit type declarations for helpers and expressions; use `eq name : type = expression;` for defining typed helpers.
Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in appropriate AADL scopes: type contracts in component types, implementation bindings in implementations.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (e.g., ms, Hz) directly in AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly exposed as typed features.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless the corresponding `with` clauses are present in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing packages or property sets
Implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
